name: option-handling
instruction:
Add a new function `grade_report(students: List[Student], target: String) -> String` that:
1. Uses `find_by_name` to look up the student
2. If found, returns `"${name}: ${grade} (${score})"` where score is converted to string
3. If not found, returns `"not found"`
Also add a new function `top_per_grade(students: List[Student], target_grade: String) -> Option[String]` that:
1. Filters students to those matching `target_grade`
2. Sorts by score descending (sort_by score, then reverse)
3. Returns `some(name)` for the first student, or `none` if no students match
Keep all existing functions unchanged.
layer3_hint: Available: `list.find(xs, fn(x) => bool)` returns `Option[A]`. Use `match` on Option: `match opt { some(x) => ..., none => ... }`. `list.filter(xs, fn(x) => bool)` filters. `list.sort_by(xs, fn(x) => key)` sorts ascending. `list.reverse(xs)` reverses. `list.first(xs)` returns `Option[A]`. `list.map(xs, fn(x) => y)` transforms. `int.to_string(n)` converts Int to String. `${}` for string interpolation.
